article.title: Huawei Launches Pura X Max, the First Wide-Format Foldable, Beating Samsung and Apple to a New Smartphone Category
article.summary: Huawei unveiled the Pura X Max on April 20 in Guangzhou, the first foldable smartphone to open horizontally into a roughly 2:1 paper-shaped display, undercutting expected wide-fold launches from Samsung and Apple later this year.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Huawei launched the Pura X Max on April 20, 2026 at a Guangzhou product event, positioning the device as the industry's first horizontally wide foldable smartphone. Unlike the book-style foldables that have defined the category since 2019, the Pura X Max opens into a near-square 7.7-inch display rather than a tall rectangle, a form factor [9to5Google described](https://9to5google.com/2026/04/13/huawei-pura-x-max-wide-foldable/) as kicking off the wide foldable trend ahead of competing devices expected from Samsung and Apple.

The launch extends a category Huawei opened with the original Pura X in March 2025, and lands at a moment when premium smartphones are driving a disproportionate share of activity in a contracting Chinese market.

## What We Know

The Pura X Max unfolds into a 7.7-inch internal LTPO OLED display and folds shut against a 5.4-inch external screen, with both panels running at up to 120Hz, [9to5Google reported](https://9to5google.com/2026/04/13/huawei-pura-x-max-wide-foldable/). The internal panel uses an aspect ratio close to 2:1 — wider and shorter than the roughly 22:18 inner displays on Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold line — giving the device a paper-like geometry when open and a stout, almost square outer screen when closed.

Pricing in China starts at 10,999 yuan, or approximately $1,610, for the 12GB/256GB configuration, [9to5Google reported](https://9to5google.com/2026/04/13/huawei-pura-x-max-wide-foldable/). Huawei is offering memory and storage variants up to 16GB/1TB, with the device available in five colors.

The Pura X Max is the direct successor to the Pura X, which Huawei launched in March 2025 as the first device running HarmonyOS 5, [CNBC reported](https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/20/huawei-pura-x-launch-specs-price-harmonyos-5.html). That earlier model already broke from convention with a 16:10 internal aspect ratio on a 6.3-inch screen, opening a small but distinct niche between traditional candybar phones and book-style foldables. The original Pura X started at 7,499 yuan, roughly $1,037 at launch, [CNBC reported](https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/20/huawei-pura-x-launch-specs-price-harmonyos-5.html).

Huawei subsequently cut Pura X prices by up to 800 yuan over a single weekend in late 2025 as Samsung and Apple were reported to be developing wide-format foldables of their own, [the South China Morning Post reported](https://www.scmp.com/tech/tech-trends/article/3337349/huawei-cuts-pura-x-prices-samsung-and-apple-foldables-loom). The same report noted that Huawei held nearly 70 percent of the Chinese foldable market in the third quarter of 2025, citing IDC data.

The Pura X Max runs HarmonyOS rather than Android, continuing the operating-system separation that began with the Pura X. The original device shipped without code from the open-source version of Google's Android, [CNBC reported](https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/20/huawei-pura-x-launch-specs-price-harmonyos-5.html), and was the first to ship with HarmonyOS 5, also known as HarmonyOS Next. The Pura X also integrated Huawei's Xiaoyi AI assistant, which drew on both Huawei's own models and DeepSeek-built models, [according to CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/20/huawei-pura-x-launch-specs-price-harmonyos-5.html).

## Why the Form Factor Matters

The wide foldable design solves a specific complaint about book-style foldables: when closed, the outer cover screen is unusually narrow, making typing and one-handed use awkward. By rotating the fold axis 90 degrees, the Pura X Max produces a cover screen with conventional phone-like proportions while still expanding into a tablet-class internal display.

The original Pura X was already structured around a 16:10 internal panel that resembled a compact tablet rather than an unfolded phone, [the South China Morning Post noted](https://www.scmp.com/tech/tech-trends/article/3337349/huawei-cuts-pura-x-prices-samsung-and-apple-foldables-loom). The same report cited supply-chain expectations that Samsung and Apple were each preparing foldables with similar wide screens approaching a 4:3 aspect ratio, framing the category as the next contested form factor in premium smartphones.

That positioning explains the Pura X Max's competitive timing. Samsung's wide-format Galaxy Z Fold variant is widely expected to debut at a summer Unpacked event, and Apple's first foldable iPhone has been reported as a fall release. By shipping in April, Huawei has staked an early claim on a design language that two of the industry's largest players are preparing to enter.

## Market Context

The launch arrives in a contracting but premium-tilted Chinese smartphone market. The original Pura X line was credited as a contributor to Huawei's commercial momentum: in the fourth quarter of 2024, Huawei's domestic market share rose to 16.2 percent from 13.7 percent a year earlier, while Apple's slipped from 20 percent to 17.4 percent over the same period, [CNBC reported](https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/20/huawei-pura-x-launch-specs-price-harmonyos-5.html).

Huawei has been pricing its foldables aggressively against the prospect of competition. The late-2025 price cut on the Pura X — up to 800 yuan in a single weekend — was explicitly framed by [the South China Morning Post](https://www.scmp.com/tech/tech-trends/article/3337349/huawei-cuts-pura-x-prices-samsung-and-apple-foldables-loom) as a defensive response to looming Samsung and Apple entries.

## What We Don't Know

Global availability of the Pura X Max remains unconfirmed. The original Pura X launched in China only, and Huawei has not announced an international rollout for the Max. U.S. trade restrictions, which constrain Huawei's access to advanced Western chips and Google services, continue to shape the company's go-to-market strategy.

Independent verification of HarmonyOS app-ecosystem maturity is also limited outside China. The Pura X shipped without Google Play Services and relies on Huawei's own AppGallery and the HarmonyOS application stack, an arrangement [CNBC reported](https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/20/huawei-pura-x-launch-specs-price-harmonyos-5.html) at the original device's launch. Whether the Pura X Max's wide-format display drives meaningful adoption among third-party developers — particularly for productivity and reading apps that benefit from the paper-like geometry — will be a meaningful test of HarmonyOS as a platform for new form factors.

It is also unclear how durable the wide foldable category will prove if Samsung's and Apple's planned entries materialize on their reported timelines. The Pura X Max ships with first-mover advantage but limited global reach; competing devices may arrive later but with broader market access and existing app ecosystems.
